body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: orientation classifier builder report
body.intention: I intend to verify orientation classifier reduces orientation token burn without introducing wrong-file misdirections.
body.description:
**What changed:** DS/CC session start uses graph-tree classifier to produce builder report instead of exploratory grep/find/read.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) Classifier misidentifies relevant files → DS works on wrong files silently. (2) Builder report confidence is overweighted — DS skips searching for files not in report. (3) Code_index staleness causes wrong results when file was recently renamed/moved.
**Signals to watch:** Ticket close notes: did DS work on the right files? Token counts before/after (cost_usd per ticket). Builder report accuracy — files listed vs files actually touched.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-26 or after 5 tickets have used the classifier.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate as occurred/clear/partial and close
**Completion criteria:** Annotated with outcome and closed.
